unit.name: VAE latent-variable model and Gaussian encoder/decoder parameterization
unit.description: Introduces the latent-variable generative story behind VAEs and explains how the encoder and decoder output distributions rather than single vectors.
unit.summary: The formal VAE setup assumes each training image x^(i) was generated from an unobserved latent vector z^(i), with the latent prior usually chosen to be a simple unit Gaussian so that sampling is easy after training [ts=3206s] [ts=3230s] [ts=3274s]. A direct maximum-likelihood treatment would write p(x) by marginalizing over z, but the necessary integral over the decoder's neural-network input is intractable in high dimensions [ts=3315s] [ts=3365s] [ts=3410s]. The core variational trick is therefore to introduce a second neural network q_phi(z|x) to approximate the intractable posterior over latent codes while the decoder p_theta(x|z) models data conditioned on z [ts=3475s] [ts=3526s] [ts=3535s]. To make those networks output distributions, the lecture constrains them to Gaussian families: the decoder predicts a mean image with fixed diagonal variance, and the encoder predicts both a mean and a diagonal covariance for z [ts=3564s] [ts=3599s] [ts=3610s]. With a fixed decoder variance, maximizing Gaussian log-likelihood reduces to minimizing L2 reconstruction error, which explains the familiar reconstruction term that appears later in the VAE objective [ts=3664s] [ts=3738s].

key_points.text: The VAE generative story assumes each observed x was produced from an unobserved latent vector z.
A simple prior such as a unit Gaussian over z makes latent sampling possible after training.
Directly marginalizing z to compute p(x) is intractable because the decoder neural network makes the integral over z impossible to compute exactly.
VAEs introduce an encoder network q_phi(z|x) to approximate the posterior while a decoder network p_theta(x|z) models data generation.
The encoder outputs a Gaussian mean and diagonal covariance for z, while the decoder outputs Gaussian parameters for x.
With fixed decoder variance, Gaussian log-likelihood becomes equivalent to L2 reconstruction.
